data: A challenging final round across history, science, sports, geography, and culture.
Which scientist proposed the three laws of motion in 'Principia Mathematica'?
The same scientist is associated with universal gravitation.
Isaac Newton presented the three laws of motion in 'Philosophiae Naturalis Principia Mathematica.' For further study, review classical mechanics.
The site is high in the Andes of Peru.
Machu Picchu was built by the Inca civilization. For further study, explore Andean history and Inca engineering.
The Suez Canal connects the Mediterranean Sea and the Red Sea.
It is a major shipping route through Egypt.
The Suez Canal links the Mediterranean Sea with the Red Sea. For further study, review global trade routes and canal history.
The process by which liquid water becomes vapor is _____.
It is part of the water cycle.
Evaporation is the process of liquid water becoming water vapor. For further study, review phase changes and the water cycle.
Which composer wrote the four-concerto set known as 'The Four Seasons'?
He was an Italian Baroque composer.
Antonio Vivaldi composed 'The Four Seasons.' For further study, review Baroque music and programmatic composition.
Which country won the first FIFA World Cup in 1930?
The host nation won the tournament.
Uruguay hosted and won the first FIFA World Cup in 1930. For further study, review early World Cup history.
Which author created the detective Sherlock Holmes?
The stories often feature Dr. Watson.
Arthur Conan Doyle created Sherlock Holmes. For further study, review the development of detective fiction.
The element carbon is found in diamonds and graphite.
They are different forms of the same element.
Diamonds and graphite are allotropes of carbon. For further study, review crystal structures and allotropes.
Which treaty ended the American Revolutionary War?
The Treaty of Paris of 1783 ended the American Revolutionary War. For further study, review the diplomacy of the American Revolution.
Which island is shared by Haiti and the Dominican Republic?
It is the second-largest island in the Caribbean.
Hispaniola is shared by Haiti and the Dominican Republic. For further study, review Caribbean geography.
The ancient trade route network linking East and West is known as the Silk _____.
Its name refers to a valuable textile.
The Silk Road connected regions of Asia, the Middle East, Africa, and Europe through trade. For further study, explore cross-cultural exchange along trade routes.
Which telescope, launched in 1990, transformed public images of deep space?
It shares a name with astronomer Edwin Hubble.
The Hubble Space Telescope launched in 1990 and produced many influential space images. For further study, review space telescope missions.
Harder entertainment trivia for film, music, TV, awards, and franchise fans.
Which film won the first Academy Award for Best Animated Feature?
The category was first awarded for films released in 2001.
'Shrek' won the first Oscar for Best Animated Feature. For further study, review Academy Award category history.
Which artist's real name was Prince Rogers Nelson?
His stage name was also his given first name.
Prince was born Prince Rogers Nelson. For further study, explore the Minneapolis sound and Prince's discography.
'The Empire Strikes Back' was released before 'Return of the Jedi.'
The original trilogy's middle chapter came out in 1980.
'The Empire Strikes Back' was released in 1980, before 'Return of the Jedi' in 1983. For further study, review Star Wars release order versus story chronology.
The fictional coffee shop in 'Friends' is Central _____.
The name is a coffee pun.
The coffee shop is Central Perk. For further study, compare recurring sitcom locations and how they anchor ensemble shows.
Which director made both 'Jaws' and 'E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial'?
He is also associated with Indiana Jones.
Steven Spielberg directed both 'Jaws' and 'E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial.' For further study, trace Spielberg's blockbuster influence.
Which novel introduced the character Katniss Everdeen?
Katniss Everdeen is the protagonist of 'The Hunger Games.' For further study, review 2000s young adult dystopian fiction.
Which band released the album 'OK Computer'?
The band is also known for 'Paranoid Android.'
Radiohead released 'OK Computer' in 1997. For further study, explore 1990s alternative rock landmarks.
The character Gollum appears in 'The Lord of the Rings.'
He is obsessed with the One Ring.
Gollum is a major character in J.R.R. Tolkien's Middle-earth stories. For further study, review the role of the One Ring across the trilogy.
Which actor played Jack Dawson in 'Titanic'?
He later won an Oscar for 'The Revenant.'
Leonardo DiCaprio played Jack Dawson in 'Titanic.' For further study, compare late-1990s blockbuster star turns.
Which TV series includes the fictional continent of Westeros?
It is based on George R.R. Martin's fantasy novels.
Westeros is a major setting in 'Game of Thrones.' For further study, review fantasy worldbuilding in television adaptations.
The fictional African nation in 'Black Panther' is _____.
It is technologically advanced and rich in vibranium.
Wakanda is the fictional home of Black Panther. For further study, explore how superhero settings shape character identity.
Which musical features the song 'My Shot'?
'My Shot' is from the musical 'Hamilton.' For further study, review modern Broadway cast albums and historical musicals.
Short, breezy questions in the spirit of a daily mini puzzle. Built for warming up.
Tropical orange-fleshed stone fruit, five letters:
Mango — five letters, fits the clue.
Largest planet in our solar system:
Jupiter is the largest, with a mass greater than all other planets combined.
Number of sides on a hexagon:
Hex- = six. Octagon has eight, pentagon has five.
Which of these is a primary color?
The three primary colors of light are red, green, and blue; in paint they are red, yellow, and blue. Yellow is the only primary in this list.
Rhode Island is about 1,545 square miles.
The yen has been Japan's currency since 1871.
Tallest mountain on Earth, measured from sea level:
Africa, Antarctica, Asia, Australia, Europe, North America, and South America.
Taste your way through dishes, languages, landmarks, festivals, and traditions.
Sushi is most closely associated with which country?
Tokyo is one of its major food capitals.
Sushi is strongly associated with Japanese cuisine.
Which language is primarily spoken in Brazil?
Portuguese is the primary language of Brazil.
The Taj Mahal is located in India.
The Taj Mahal is a famous mausoleum in Agra, India.
The Italian city famous for canals and gondolas is _____.
Venice is famous for its canals and gondolas.
Which festival is often called the Festival of Lights?
It is widely celebrated by Hindus, Sikhs, Jains, and others.
Diwali is commonly known as the Festival of Lights.
The rice dish is especially associated with Valencia.
Paella originated in Spain, particularly the Valencia region.
Which city is home to the Eiffel Tower?
It is the capital of France.
The Eiffel Tower is in Paris, France.
Spanish and Portuguese are both Romance languages.
Spanish and Portuguese are Romance languages descended from Latin.
Which country is known for the traditional garment called the kimono?
The kimono is a traditional Japanese garment.
Which drink is traditionally central to a Japanese tea ceremony?
It is a powdered green tea.
Matcha is the powdered green tea used in the Japanese tea ceremony.
A quick round of everyday facts, famous places, nature, and classic knowledge.
What is the capital city of Canada?
It is in Ontario, but it is not the province's largest city.
Ottawa is Canada's capital city. Toronto is Ontario's capital and Canada's largest city.
Which planet is known as the Red Planet?
Its rusty color comes from iron oxide on the surface.
Mars is called the Red Planet because iron-rich dust gives it a reddish appearance.
The Pacific Ocean is the largest ocean on Earth.
It covers more area than all land on Earth combined.
The Pacific Ocean is Earth's largest and deepest ocean.
The same playwright wrote 'Macbeth' and 'Romeo and Juliet.'
William Shakespeare wrote 'Hamlet' around the turn of the 17th century.
The tallest mountain above sea level is Mount _____.
Mount Everest is the tallest mountain above sea level.
Which gas do plants absorb from the air during photosynthesis?
It is the gas humans exhale as a waste product.
Plants use carbon dioxide, water, and light energy to make sugars during photosynthesis.
How many sides does a triangle have?
A triangle has three sides and three angles.
The Mona Lisa is displayed at the Louvre Museum in Paris.
The museum is famous for its glass pyramid entrance.
Leonardo da Vinci's Mona Lisa is displayed at the Louvre Museum in Paris.
Major moments, people, inventions, and turning points from world history.
Which ancient civilization built the pyramids at Giza?
The pyramids stand near the Nile River.
The ancient Egyptians built the pyramids at Giza.
Who was the first president of the United States?
He also commanded the Continental Army.
George Washington was the first president of the United States.
The printing press helped spread books and ideas more widely in Europe.
Think of Gutenberg and movable type.
Movable type printing made books easier to produce and helped ideas circulate more widely.
The wall that divided a German city during the Cold War was the Berlin _____.
The Berlin Wall divided East and West Berlin from 1961 until 1989.
Which empire was ruled by Julius Caesar shortly before it became an empire under Augustus?
Julius Caesar was a central figure in the late Roman Republic before Augustus became Rome's first emperor.
The storming of the Bastille happened that year.
Which explorer's 1492 voyage reached the Caribbean while sailing for Spain?
His voyage is often connected with the phrase 'in 1492.'
Christopher Columbus reached the Caribbean in 1492 while sailing under the Spanish crown.
The Magna Carta was signed in medieval England.
It is associated with King John in 1215.
The Magna Carta was agreed to in England in 1215.
Which war ended with the Treaty of Versailles in 1919?
The treaty followed the armistice of 1918.
The Treaty of Versailles was one of the major peace treaties ending World War I.
Who was the first woman to win a Nobel Prize?
She later won a second Nobel Prize in a different science.
Marie Curie was the first woman to win a Nobel Prize.
A breezy mix of movies, music, TV, and entertainment staples.
Which movie features the line 'May the Force be with you'?
It is a space saga with Jedi and lightsabers.
'May the Force be with you' is one of the signature lines from the Star Wars franchise.
He was often called the King of Pop.
The TV series 'Friends' is set primarily in New York City.
The characters often gather at Central Perk.
'Friends' follows a group of friends living in New York City.
What is the name of the cowboy toy in 'Toy Story'?
His pull-string voice is a big part of the first movie.
Woody is the cowboy doll and one of the central characters in 'Toy Story.'
The wizarding school in the Harry Potter series is called _____.
Its four houses include Gryffindor and Slytherin.
Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry is the main school in the Harry Potter series.
Which superhero is also known as the Dark Knight?
Batman is often called the Dark Knight.
Which animated film features the song 'Let It Go'?
The song is performed by Queen Elsa.
The Beatles were originally from Liverpool, England.
Their hometown is strongly associated with Merseybeat.
The Beatles formed in Liverpool, England.
From the 1992 inaugural season to today — how well do you know the EPL?
Who is the Premier League's all-time top scorer?
Alan Shearer scored 260 Premier League goals, mostly with Newcastle United.
Which club won the inaugural Premier League title in the 1992–93 season?
Manchester United, managed by Sir Alex Ferguson, won the first ever Premier League.
Erling Haaland joined Manchester City in 2022 from which club?
Haaland transferred from Borussia Dortmund and won the Golden Boot in his first PL season.
Which stadium is home to Liverpool FC?
Anfield has been Liverpool's home since 1892.
Who has won the most Premier League Golden Boots?
Both Henry and Shearer are tied
Thierry Henry and Alan Shearer are tied on four Golden Boots each.
Salah captains the Egyptian national team.
Brighton & Hove Albion, named for their coastal home.
Which player holds the record for most Premier League appearances?
Gareth Barry made 653 PL appearances across Aston Villa, Manchester City, Everton, and West Brom.
Which manager led Leicester City to their fairytale 2015–16 title?
Claudio Ranieri's Leicester won the title at 5000-1 preseason odds.
What is the largest stadium currently used in the Premier League by capacity?
Which club won four Premier League titles in a row from 2020–21 to 2023–24?
Manchester City's four consecutive titles is a PL record.
Newcastle United play their home games at:
St James' Park has been Newcastle's home since 1892.
Who is Arsenal's all-time leading goal scorer in the Premier League era?
Thierry Henry is Arsenal's all-time top scorer with 228 goals across all competitions.
In what year was the Premier League founded?
The Premier League was founded in February 1992.
Which goalkeeper has the most Premier League clean sheets all-time?
Petr Čech finished his career with 202 PL clean sheets, a league record.
A tougher trivia set about milestones, unusual facts, and notable firsts.
Who was the first person to walk on the Moon?
He spoke about one small step.
Neil Armstrong became the first person to walk on the Moon in 1969. For further study, review the Apollo 11 mission.
Which country gifted the Statue of Liberty to the United States?
The gift commemorated friendship across the Atlantic.
France gave the Statue of Liberty to the United States. For further study, explore the statue's design and dedication history.
The first modern Olympic Games were held in Athens.
The location connected the modern games to ancient Greece.
The first modern Olympic Games were held in Athens in 1896. For further study, review the revival of the Olympics.
The first artificial satellite launched into orbit was _____.
It was launched by the Soviet Union in 1957.
Sputnik 1 was the first artificial satellite. For further study, review the beginning of the Space Age.
Which animal is known for having fingerprints that can resemble human fingerprints?
It is a tree-dwelling marsupial from Australia.
Koalas have fingerprints that can look surprisingly similar to human fingerprints. For further study, compare convergent traits in animals.
Which element has the atomic number 1?
Hydrogen has atomic number 1. For further study, review the structure of the periodic table.
Which city hosted the first widely recognized modern World's Fair in 1851?
It was held in the Crystal Palace.
London hosted the Great Exhibition of 1851. For further study, review World's Fairs and industrial-era display culture.
Venus rotates in the opposite direction from most planets in the solar system.
Venus has retrograde rotation compared with most planets. For further study, review planetary rotation and axial tilt.
Which book is often cited as the best-selling novel of all time?
It was written by Miguel de Cervantes.
'Don Quixote' is often cited as the best-selling novel of all time. For further study, review early modern European literature.
Which metal is liquid at room temperature?
It shares its name with a planet.
Mercury is a metal that is liquid at room temperature. For further study, review states of matter and metallic elements.
The world's largest coral reef system is the Great Barrier _____.
It lies off Australia's northeast coast.
The Great Barrier Reef is the world's largest coral reef system. For further study, review reef ecosystems and marine biodiversity.
Who was the first woman to fly solo across the Atlantic Ocean?
Amelia Earhart was the first woman to fly solo across the Atlantic Ocean. For further study, review early aviation milestones.
Explore space, biology, chemistry, weather, and everyday physics.
What is the chemical symbol for gold?
It comes from the Latin word 'aurum.'
Which organ pumps blood through the human body?
The heart pumps blood through the circulatory system.
The force that pulls objects toward Earth is _____.
It also keeps planets in orbit around the Sun.
Gravity is the force of attraction between masses.
Which part of a plant carries out most photosynthesis?
Leaves contain chlorophyll and are the main site of photosynthesis in many plants.
Sound travels faster in water than in air.
Particles are closer together in water.
Sound generally travels faster in water than in air because water transmits vibrations more efficiently.
What is the largest planet in our solar system?
It is famous for the Great Red Spot.
Jupiter is the largest planet in our solar system.
Which scale is commonly used to measure earthquake magnitude?
It is named after a seismologist.
The Richter scale is a well-known scale for earthquake magnitude, though modern measurements often use related moment magnitude scales.
Which blood cells help fight infection?
Their name points to their color category, not their job.
White blood cells are part of the immune system and help fight infections.
It has two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom.
Water is made of two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom, giving it the formula H2O.
What type of celestial object is the Sun?
It produces light and heat through nuclear fusion.
The Sun is a star at the center of our solar system.
Classic sports trivia across trophies, rules, teams, and famous athletes.
How many players are on the court for one basketball team during normal play?
Think of the five traditional positions.
A basketball team has five players on the court during standard play.
Which tournament awards the FIFA World Cup trophy?
FIFA governs the world's most popular ball sport.
The FIFA World Cup is the top international soccer tournament.
A tennis match can be played as singles or doubles.
Doubles puts two players on each side.
Tennis can be played one-on-one as singles or two-on-two as doubles.
In baseball, how many strikes usually make an out?
A batter is usually out after three strikes.
The Olympic Games are symbolized by five interlocking _____.
They appear in blue, yellow, black, green, and red.
The Olympic symbol is five interlocking rings.
Which sport is associated with Wimbledon?
The event is famous for grass courts.
Wimbledon is one of tennis's four Grand Slam tournaments.
Which athlete is famous for winning eight gold medals in swimming at the 2008 Olympics?
He is the most decorated Olympian in history.
Michael Phelps won eight swimming gold medals at the 2008 Beijing Olympics.
In golf, the goal is to complete each hole in as few strokes as possible.
Lower scores are better in golf.
Travel through countries, rivers, cities, landmarks, and maps.
It is strongly associated with ancient Egypt.
The Nile River flows through Cairo and has shaped Egyptian history for millennia.
Which country is both a continent and a country?
It is surrounded by the Indian and Pacific Oceans.
Australia is commonly described as both a country and a continent.
The imaginary line at 0 degrees latitude is the _____.
It divides Earth into Northern and Southern Hemispheres.
The equator is the line of 0 degrees latitude.
Which mountain range separates much of Europe from Asia?
The range runs north-south through western Russia.
The Ural Mountains are commonly used as part of the boundary between Europe and Asia.
Its major islands include Honshu, Hokkaido, Kyushu, and Shikoku.
Japan is an archipelago made up of thousands of islands.
Which desert is the largest hot desert in the world?
It stretches across much of North Africa.
The Sahara is the world's largest hot desert.
Which city is home to the Colosseum?
It was the heart of the ancient Roman Empire.
The Colosseum is in Rome, Italy.
Which country has the longest coastline in the world?
Its Arctic islands add a huge amount of shoreline.
Canada has the world's longest coastline.
The Andes are located in South America.
They run along the western edge of the continent.
The Andes are a major mountain range along South America's western side.
Which sea lies between Europe and Africa?
Ancient Greek, Roman, Egyptian, and Phoenician trade crossed it.
The Mediterranean Sea lies between southern Europe and northern Africa.
